title: Weekly Markets Monitor - Red Friday
gist: World Gold Council's 8 June 2026 weekly note recaps a broad-based 'Red Friday' (5 June) sell-off driven by a stronger-than-expected US jobs report that pulled forward Fed hike expectations to December, vulnerable technical setups in gold/silver/bitcoin, and stretched hedge-fund/CTA positioning. Only the USD and CHF rose; weekend Iranian strikes on Israel then pushed oil nearly 5% higher.

insights.claim: On Friday 5 June 2026, price weakness across markets was broad-based, with only the US dollar and Swiss franc rising while gold, silver, bitcoin and US equities fell.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A simultaneous risk-off move where only USD and CHF held up signals a liquidity/positioning unwind rather than a single-asset story; watch for follow-through across correlated longs.
insights.quote: Only the USD and CHF managed to buck the trend.

insights.claim: World Gold Council attributes the 5 June sell-off to vulnerable technical setups in gold, silver and bitcoin, extended hedge-fund and CTA positioning in US equities (per Vanda Research), reported levered-ETF rebalancing, and a stronger US jobs report.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: The drop was positioning- and technical-driven, not a fundamental regime shift — mean-reversion is plausible once leveraged longs are flushed.
insights.quote: extended positioning from hedge funds and Commodity Trading Advisors (US equities – according to Vanda Research), reports of levered ETF rebalancing

themes.theme: US labor market and Fed path

insights.claim: A stronger-than-expected US employment report raised expectations of tighter monetary policy and pulled forward Fed hike expectations to December 2026.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A pulled-forward December hike repricing pressures rate-sensitive and non-yielding assets like gold and bitcoin; track Fed-dated futures for confirmation.
insights.quote: a stronger-than-expected employment report in the US – which pulled forward Fed hike expectations to December

insights.claim: Global signals were mixed in early June 2026: Eurozone activity weakened while inflation rose, China showed divergence between large- and small-firm activity, Japan's real wages continued to rise, and India's Q1 GDP beat expectations.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: Cross-region divergence (stagflationary Eurozone, resilient India/Japan) complicates a single global macro trade and argues for selective regional positioning.
insights.quote: Eurozone activity weakened and inflation rose, China showed divergence between activity of large and small firms, Japan’s real wages continued to rise, and India’s Q1 GDP beat expectations.

insights.claim: Iranian strikes against Israel over the weekend of 6-7 June 2026 sent oil prices almost 5% higher as of the note's writing, compounding market uncertainty.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A ~5% oil spike on fresh Iran-Israel escalation is an inflationary and risk-premium impulse — supportive for energy and potentially gold as a hedge.
insights.quote: Iranian strikes against Israel over the weekend have compounded the uncertainty, having sent oil prices almost 5% higher as of writing.

insights.claim: Ahead of the sell-off, Polymarket priced a 72% probability of a Strait of Hormuz peace deal by year-end, reflecting lofty expectations that the conflict was nearing resolution.
insights.speaker: World Gold Council
insights.so_what: A 72% priced-in peace probability shows complacency that the weekend escalation can violently reprice — geopolitical risk premia were too low going in.
insights.quote: Polymarket had a 72% probability of a peace deal by year-end priced in
